commit_message:
[otbn,dv] Update model for integrity checking on ISPRs

Signed-off-by: Andreas Kurth <adk@lowrisc.org>
pull_body:
These are my local changes from master which produce the bug (#12067) on the following seeds:

$ util/dvsim/dvsim.py hw/ip/pwm/dv/pwm_sim_cfg.hjson -i pwm_rand_output  --waves --verbosity d --fixed-seed 603746614
$ util/dvsim/dvsim.py hw/ip/pwm/dv/pwm_sim_cfg.hjson -i pwm_rand_output  --waves --verbosity d --fixed-seed 825849404
$ util/dvsim/dvsim.py hw/ip/pwm/dv/pwm_sim_cfg.hjson -i pwm_rand_output  --waves --verbosity d --fixed-seed 1950056355
$ util/dvsim/dvsim.py hw/ip/pwm/dv/pwm_sim_cfg.hjson -i pwm_rand_output  --waves --verbosity d --fixed-seed 2688175697
$ util/dvsim/dvsim.py hw/ip/pwm/dv/pwm_sim_cfg.hjson -i pwm_rand_output  --waves --verbosity d --fixed-seed 3509102803

The waves indicate that despite the bit set in reg_en, the output on one of the set channels is not toggled on.

Signed-off-by: Jason Hoddinett <jason.hoddinett.ensilica@opentitan.org>
Issue title: [pwm/bugfix] Channel set in reg_en not producing output
Issue Body: There appears to be leftover items in exp_item_q, being generated by the scoreboard. These additional items remain un-compared as the monitor does not produce any items for comparison.    The waves indicate that the reg_en is being asserted for the bit, however, that bit is not being enabled on the output.    To replicate this issue, I have a PR with my following environment changes #12066     $ util/dvsim/dvsim.py hw/ip/pwm/dv/pwm_sim_cfg.hjson -i pwm_rand_output --waves --verbosity d --fixed-seed 603746614  $ util/dvsim/dvsim.py hw/ip/pwm/dv/pwm_sim_cfg.hjson -i pwm_rand_output --waves --verbosity d --fixed-seed 825849404  $ util/dvsim/dvsim.py hw/ip/pwm/dv/pwm_sim_cfg.hjson -i pwm_rand_output --waves --verbosity d --fixed-seed 1950056355  $ util/dvsim/dvsim.py hw/ip/pwm/dv/pwm_sim_cfg.hjson -i pwm_rand_output --waves --verbosity d --fixed-seed 2688175697  $ util/dvsim/dvsim.py hw/ip/pwm/dv/pwm_sim_cfg.hjson -i pwm_rand_output --waves --verbosity d --fixed-seed 3509102803
Comments: martin-lueker: Hi @jason-hoddinett,     My apologies for taking so long to get to this.  I've looked at the first seed (603746614) and it seems that the problem there may be that the duty cycle for the 'unresponsive' channel is effectively 0, or at least so small given that the low DC_RESN value (1).   That means that for such a small value of DUTY_CYCLE[5].a = 0x054B, where the 2 most significant bits are 0, the channel is effectively off.       Is the testbench set up to handle this situation?    I can look at the other seeds now to see if this pattern holds.  I'll let you know what I find. martin-lueker: A follow-up: I looked at all five of the above seeds, and they all showed a similar pattern: they all had an A-phase blink duty cycle that was relatively small, and -- when the DC_RESN is taken into account -- the effective duty cycle was 0. So in each of these seeds the "non-responsive" channels should not be pulsing at this time. jason-hoddinett: This issue has been resolved and the fix is now located and awaiting PR merge #12066
